headline: BlackRock Pivots Back to US Stocks With Strategists Seeing Iran War ‘Contained’
summary: (Bloomberg) -- BlackRock strategists have returned to an overweight view on US stocks as they see the damage to global economic growth from the Middle East conflict “likely contained.”The firm had been watching “two signposts to dial up risk-taking” after shifting to neutral as the Middle East conflict led to reducing risk a few weeks ago, strategists led by Jean Boivin, the head of the BlackRock Investment Institute, wrote in a Monday note. Those signals were moves to restart shipping via the S

headline: BlackRock raises U.S. stock outlook on earnings, Iran war outlook
summary: Investing.com -- Asset management giant BlackRock has raised its outlook for U.S. stocks, citing contained impacts from the Iran war and strong corporate earnings expectations.

raw.headline: BlackRock raises U.S. stock outlook on earnings, Iran war outlook

headline: BlackRock raises view on U.S. stocks on belief that war is over, profits are up
summary: The firm, which manages $14 trillion for clients, said in its weekly market note that it raised the rating a notch to overweight from neutral.

headline: Is It Time To Reassess BlackRock (BLK) After Its Strong Multi‑Year Share Price Run?
summary: If you are wondering whether BlackRock's current share price reflects its true value, the starting point is understanding how the market has been treating the stock lately and how that lines up with its fundamentals. BlackRock most recently closed at US$999.31, with returns of 4.2% over 7 days, 8.1% over 30 days, a year to date return of 7.9%, 14.6% over 1 year, 53.6% over 3 years, and 38.9% over 5 years. This provides a sense of how sentiment has shifted across different timeframes. Recent...

headline: Earnings Volatility Watch: This Stock Could Move 30% In A Single Day This Week
summary: Options markets are pricing post-earnings swings of up to 30% on stocks with market caps above $10 billion reporting this week, including ASML, Netflix, Taiwan Semi and Wipro.
